name: Muslim Wellness Foundation
mission: Muslim Wellness Foundation (MWF) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting healing and well-being in American Muslim communities through dialogue, education and training. The MWF approach to health is interdisciplinary, intergenerational, spiritually grounded, community based, and justice-oriented. From being the first Muslim organization in the country to offer Mental Health First Aid (MHFA) trainings to establishing the annual Black Muslim Psychology Conference (BMPC), MWF has been an outspoken leader in fostering visions for wellness and healing. Our programs demonstrate our keen awareness of the link between oppression, wellness and how communities heal, grow, thrive, and experience joy in the midst of it all. We will continue to lead the way in shifting the conversation on mental health, addiction and trauma - from one narrowly focused on stigma to a healing-centered model of collective well-being, faith, identity and belonging. Join us on this journey.
theoryOfChange: The MWF approach to health is interdisciplinary, intergenerational, spiritually grounded, community based, and justice-oriented, aiming to shift from stigma-focused models to healing-centered models of collective well-being.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate score of 54/100, reflecting a strong commitment to program spending offset by low financial liquidity and limited data transparency.
areas_for_improvement: Limited financial cushion with only 0.2 months of working capital available [1]
Low data confidence and limited third-party verification of long-term impact [2]

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization addresses the urgent global health issue of mental health within a minority context, though it currently shows low donor fit for those seeking high-transparency ratings [1]. As a sadaqah-eligible entity, it offers a specific pathway for general charitable giving within the community [5].
dimension_explanations.impact: MWF demonstrates a direct approach to capacity building through Mental Health First Aid (MHFA) trainings and the Black Muslim Psychology Conference (BMPC) [3][4]. While the organization directs 100.0% of its expenses to these programs, the overall impact score of 27/50 is tempered by low data confidence and a lack of cost-per-beneficiary metrics [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Muslim Wellness Foundation Inc is a nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting healing and emotional well-being within the American Muslim community through specialized mental health programming.
strengths: Maintains a program expense ratio of 100.0%, indicating all reported expenses are directed toward its mission [1]
Operates with high fundraising efficiency, spending $0.00 per $1 raised [2]
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Wellness Foundation (MWF) focuses on reducing stigma and providing mental health resources tailored to the unique needs of American Muslims [3]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $223,205 to support its community-based initiatives [1][2].
